source.title: How the Biblical Canon Was Formed
source.summary: The New Testament canon developed gradually through the 1st-4th centuries AD based on apostolic authorship, orthodox content, and widespread church acceptance. Unlike the Qur'an's centralized standardization, the biblical canon emerged through consensus across diverse geographic regions and independent church communities. The Muratorian Fragment (c. 170 AD) already lists most NT books, showing early widespread agreement.
